title: androsace tibetica var. kurramensis
intro: Androsace tibetica var. kurramensis, also known as Kurram Androsace, is a rare and endangered species of flowering plant belonging to the Primulaceae family. It is native to the mountainous regions of the Kurram Agency in Pakistan and is traditionally used in the field of traditional medicine due to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to boost the immune system and exhibit antiseptic properties. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers are used to prepare various herbal preparations, including infusions, decoctions, and powders, which are consumed orally or applied topically to harness its medicinal properties.
